channel.title: NPR Topics: Technology
channel.description: Latest technology news and breakthroughs in technology, science, and industry. Download the NPR Technology podcast and Technology RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: China's AI chatbots are advanced and versatile — and begging for more users
item.description: Chinese AI companies are focused less on being cutting edge and more on attracting customers. That means holiday promotions, and making chatbots useful in everyday life.
item.content:encoded:
Chinese AI companies are focused less on being cutting edge and more on attracting customers. That means holiday promotions, and making chatbots useful in everyday life.

(Image credit: ADEK BERRY/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Expert talks about the Pentagon's use of artificial intelligence
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Tristan Harris, co-founder of the Center for Humane Technology, about the use of AI by the Pentagon.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Tristan Harris, co-founder of the Center for Humane Technology, about the use of AI by the Pentagon.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: How will two landmark social media verdicts reshape how we use this technology?
item.description: Two landmark verdicts this week could reshape the way social media works. Aza Raskin, a co-founder of the Center for Humane Technology, explains the implications of the courts' decisions.
item.content:encoded: Two landmark verdicts this week could reshape the way social media works. Aza Raskin, a co-founder of the Center for Humane Technology, explains the implications of the courts' decisions.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Ma

item.title: Research points to how companies could make social media less addictive for teens
item.description: Juries in two big cases have affirmed what research is finding: The design of social media platforms is particularly compelling and hard to resist for kids. There are growing calls to change it.
item.content:encoded:
Juries in two big cases have affirmed what research is finding: The design of social media platforms is particularly compelling and hard to resist for kids. There are growing calls to change it.

(Image credit: Fiordaliso/Moment RF)
item.dc:creator: Rhitu Chatterjee

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks Trump administration's Anthropic ban
item.description: The order briefly stops the government from labeling tech company Anthropic a "supply chain risk," calling that "classic First Amendment retaliation."
item.content:encoded:
The order briefly stops the government from labeling tech company Anthropic a "supply chain risk," calling that "classic First Amendment retaliation."

(Image credit: RICCARDO MILANI/Hans Lucas)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Jury finds Meta and Google negligent in social media addiction trial
item.description: A jury found Meta and Google were to blame for the depression and anxiety of a woman who compulsively used social media as a child. The landmark verdict may influence the outcome of other lawsuits.
item.content:encoded: A jury found Meta and Google were to blame for the depression and anxiety of a woman who compulsively used social media as a child. The landmark verdict may influence the outcome of other lawsuits.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Melania Trump shares the spotlight with a robot at an education and technology event
item.description: The robot accompanied the first lady to the White House East Room for the final day of a summit she had convened with counterparts from around the world through her Fostering the Future Together global initiative.
item.content:encoded:
The robot accompanied the first lady to the White House East Room for the final day of a summit she had convened with counterparts from around the world through her Fostering the Future Together global initiative.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Jury finds Meta and Google negligent in social media harms trial
item.description: The jury ordered the companies to pay $6 million in damages over defective design. The landmark verdict may influence the outcome of 2,000 other pending lawsuits.
item.content:encoded:
The jury ordered the companies to pay $6 million in damages over defective design. The landmark verdict may influence the outcome of 2,000 other pending lawsuits.

(Image credit: Frederic J. Brown)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Your data is everywhere. The government is buying it without a warrant
item.description: Data brokers buy up huge amounts of information from cell phones and browsers to sell for targeted advertising. But the government, including ICE, also buys the data.
item.content:encoded:
Data brokers buy up huge amounts of information from cell phones and browsers to sell for targeted advertising. But the government, including ICE, also buys the data.

(Image credit: Mandel Ngan)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block

item.title: OpenAI pulls the plug on Sora, the viral AI video app that sparked deepfake concerns
item.description: OpenAI said Tuesday that it was "saying goodbye to the Sora app" and that it would share more soon about how to preserve what users already created on the app.
item.content:encoded:
OpenAI said Tuesday that it was "saying goodbye to the Sora app" and that it would share more soon about how to preserve what users already created on the app.

(Image credit: Michael Dwyer)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
